_daily_brief: Risk-on sentiment with 14 bullish signals. Biggest mover: Initial Jobless Claims, up 16K to 219K. Initial claims at 219K ▲. Oil above $99 is feeding inflation expectations — stagflation risk elevated. Key catalyst: CPI (Mar) due today.
_weekly_wrap: This week: 12 indicators improved, 12 deteriorated, 3 unchanged. Nonfarm Payrolls (MoM) moved most, up 318K to +178K. VIX also notable, down 18.4% to 19.48. Overall macro regime unchanged week-over-week. The week was defined by persistent oil-driven inflation pressure. Next week: Retail Sales (Mar) (16 Apr). These could shift the consumption picture.

_forward_look.title: Oil shock persistence
_forward_look.text: If oil holds above $99 through month-end, expect next CPI to print 2.6–2.8% — above consensus. This would push rate cut expectations to zero for H1 2026 and pressure equities toward 6,483 support.

_forward_look.title: Inflation re-acceleration
_forward_look.text: If shelter costs stay sticky (2.7%) and oil adds 0.3–0.5pp to headline CPI, expect Fed rhetoric to turn more hawkish. 10Y could test 4.59% and growth stocks underperform value.

_forward_look.title: Geopolitical de-escalation
_forward_look.text: If Iran conflict resolves and Strait reopens, oil could drop to $70–75. VIX back below 18, equities rally 5–8%. Energy stocks give back gains, consumer discretionary outperforms.
